preamble: The open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ] raises to the close front unrounded vowel [i] through vowel raising. This process eliminates the mid-vowel articulation and produces a higher, tenser vowel. This shift occurs in historical sound changes and contemporary dialect variation.

examples.note: Raising in certain phonological contexts, particularly before velars or in word-final stressed position.
Regional vowel raising in certain German dialects, particularly in southern varieties.
Historical raising in certain Romance language evolution patterns.
Historical vowel raising in Old Scandinavian → Middle Scandinavian transitions.
